title: Access Modifiers & Encapsulation
content:
# Java Interview Prep

## Q1: Explain why the Java Memory Model (JMM) Happen-Before relationship is critical for volatile variables.

### Standard Answer
Without volatile, compilers and multi-core CPUs reorder instructions and cache variables locally to optimize throughput. The JMM guarantees that a write to a volatile field happens-before every subsequent read of that same field. This forces thread reads and writes directly to main memory and inserts memory barriers to prevent compiler and CPU reorderings, ensuring thread visibility.


## Q2: What is the spatial complexity bounds of Access Modifiers & Encapsulation?

### Standard Answer
The spatial complexity scales strictly as $O(1)$ auxiliary memory unless additional arrays or dynamic heap nodes are allocated, yielding $O(N)$ total space.
